data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Rise for Second Consecutive Day Amid Safe-Haven Demand
data.description: Gold prices have increased for the second day in a row, driven by safe-haven demand. According to RTHK, spot gold briefly rose to $4,580.13 per ounce, marking a 1.9% increase, before narrowing its gains to 0.4% and hovering around $4,510. Meanwhile, New York June gold futures settled at $4,557.5, up more than 0.7%. Edited by Fang Jiali.

data.title: Republican Senators Propose Bill to Restrict Crypto Mining Equipment from China
data.description: Republican senators have introduced a new bill aimed at preventing U.S. cryptocurrency mining companies from utilizing equipment linked to China and other foreign adversaries. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing concerns over national security and the influence of foreign technology in critical industries. The proposed legislation seeks to safeguard the U.S. crypto mining sector by restricting the use of foreign-made equipment that could pose potential risks. This move reflects ongoing efforts to address vulnerabilities in the supply chain and protect domestic interests. The bill underscores the importance of ensuring that critical infrastructure remains free from foreign influence, particularly from nations considered adversarial to the United States.

data.title: European Stock Markets Rebound with Significant Gains
data.description: European stock markets saw notable gains, with major indices rising nearly 1% or more, ending a two-day losing streak. According to RTHK, the UK's FTSE 100 index expanded its gains in the afternoon, closing above the 10,000-point level at 10,127 points, marking an increase of 160 points or 1.6%. Germany's DAX index also moved upwards, closing at 22,562 points, a rise of 262 points or approximately 1.2%. Meanwhile, France's CAC index ended at 7,772 points, up by 70 points, reflecting an increase of over 0.9%.

data.title: Democratic Lawmakers Urge Ban on Federal Workers Using Prediction Markets
data.description: More than 40 Democratic lawmakers have called on U.S. regulators to prohibit federal employees from engaging in prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi. According to NS3.AI, the lawmakers sent a letter to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and federal ethics officials, emphasizing that regulations concerning nonpublic information in derivatives trading should explicitly include these platforms.

data.title: Crypto Card Payment Volume Surpasses $125 Million in November
data.description: According to NS3.AI, the monthly payment volume for crypto cards exceeded $100 million last November, marking a significant milestone in the adoption of cryptocurrency for everyday transactions. Unfolded reported that the monthly volume began near zero at the start of 2023 and later peaked above $125 million, indicating a growing trend in the use of crypto cards for payments.

data.title: AMD Emerges as Stronger Buy Amid Chip Market Selloff
data.description: AMD is being highlighted as a more favorable investment following a recent selloff in the chip market. According to NS3.AI, the selloff resulted in Intel's shares dropping by 4.5%, while AMD experienced a decline of just under 3%. The positive outlook for AMD is attributed to the anticipated launch of its Helios AI rack and expected MI450 revenue in the third quarter. In contrast, Intel faces a mixed outlook due to ongoing concerns in the CPU and PC markets.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Highlights Importance of Data in Assessing Energy's Impact on Inflation
data.description: Federal Reserve's Williams emphasized the necessity of focusing on data to evaluate how energy will influence inflation. According to ChainCatcher, this statement underscores the central bank's approach to monitoring economic indicators closely to guide its policy decisions.

data.title: CME Data: Fed Likely to Maintain Rates in April
data.description: According to Jin10, CME's "FedWatch" tool indicates a 2.6% probability of a 25 basis point rate hike by the Federal Reserve in April, while the likelihood of maintaining the current rate stands at 97.4%. Looking ahead to June, there is a 5% chance of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut, a 92.5% probability of rates remaining unchanged, and a 2.5% chance of a cumulative 25 basis point rate hike.

data.title: U.S. Stock Indices Show Mixed Performance Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
U.S. stock indices displayed mixed results as uncertainties in the Middle East persisted. According to RTHK, U.S. President Donald Trump issued a new threat to Iran, stating that if Iran does not open the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. will target Iran's oil wells and power plants. Meanwhile, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell noted that the current energy shock's impact is still uncertain, and the Federal Reserve does not need to take immediate action as inflation expectations appear stable, with authorities closely monitoring the situation.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average stabilized after two consecutive days of decline, initially rising by about 460 points before briefly turning negative. It closed at 45,216 points, up 49 points, or 0.11%. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices initially rose but then fell, marking three consecutive days of decline and reaching their lowest closing levels in nearly eight months. The S&P 500 closed at 6,343 points, down 25 points, or nearly 0.4%, while the Nasdaq ended at 20,794 points, down 153 points, or over 0.7%.

Memory chip stocks experienced sharp declines, with SanDisk, Western Digital, and Micron falling between 7% and nearly 10%. Major technology stocks showed varied performance, with Nvidia dropping over 1%, Tesla nearly 2%, and Intel 4.5%, while Microsoft and Amazon rose by nearly 1%.

data.title: Washington State Implements New Tax on Million-Dollar Incomes
data.description: Washington State has introduced a new tax targeting individuals with incomes exceeding $1 million. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that this move aims to generate additional revenue for the state, which will be used to fund various public services and infrastructure projects. The tax is expected to impact a small percentage of the state's population, primarily affecting high-income earners. Proponents argue that the tax will help address income inequality and provide necessary resources for community development. However, critics express concerns about potential negative effects on economic growth and the possibility of wealthy individuals relocating to states with more favorable tax policies. The implementation of this tax marks a significant shift in Washington's approach to taxation, as it previously relied heavily on sales and property taxes. The new tax is part of a broader trend among states seeking to increase revenue by targeting high-income earners.

data.title: CFTC to Consider Sports League Input on Prediction Market Contracts
data.description: CFTC Chairman Michael Selig announced that the agency will place significant emphasis on the opinions of sports leagues when assessing prediction market contracts. According to NS3.AI, the NFL has communicated with platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, advising them to steer clear of contracts linked to outcomes that can be easily manipulated or predetermined.

data.title: US senators question SEC leadership changes and decision to drop the case against Justin Sun.
data.description: U.S. Senator Richard Blumenthal wrote to SEC Chairman Paul Atkins, questioning the short tenure of former enforcement chief Margaret Ryan and the reasons for her departure, and expressing concern over the SEC's dropping of charges against Tron founder Justin Sun. Ryan took office in September 2025 and left in March of this year. Blumenthal stated in the letter that her sudden departure raises questions, especially given reports that senior SEC officials interfered with enforcement, limiting progress in some crypto company cases. According to media reports, Ryan attempted to strengthen investigations into fraud cases, including those involving individuals associated with President Trump, but faced opposition from some Republican senior members of the committee. The disagreement was particularly pronounced in the Justin Sun case. Previously, during the Biden administration, the SEC accused Sun and his affiliates of conducting unregistered securities offerings through TRX and BTT tokens, manipulating the market through wash trading, and paying celebrities for undisclosed promotions. Under the current administration, the SEC has dropped several enforcement cases against crypto companies, including Coinbase, Kraken, and Binance, raising concerns about a shift in regulatory direction.

data.title: Democrats Urge CFTC to Address Insider Trading in Prediction Markets
data.description: More than 40 Democratic lawmakers have called on the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and U.S. ethics officials to issue warnings to federal employees regarding the illegality of insider trading in prediction market derivatives. According to NS3.AI, the March 29 letter highlighted concerns over suspicious bets on contracts related to military actions in Venezuela and Iran, a press secretary's speech, and the dismissal of Kristi Noem. This request coincides with the CFTC's efforts to develop new policies for prediction markets, including platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi.

data.title: The CFTC will tighten regulations on forecast market contracts, taking into account the NFL's feedback.
data.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) stated that it will consider the opinions of sports leagues when assessing the risks of forecast market contracts, following the NFL's call for platforms to tighten related trading rules. The NFL has reportedly sent a letter to Kalshi and Polymarket, requesting them to avoid listing contracts that are easily manipulated or whose outcomes can be predetermined, such as contracts related to single game events (e.g., whether a first pass is missed, whether a free throw is made), draft results, roster decisions, and broadcast-related content. The league also specifically pointed out that markets related to foul calls and player injuries could create incentives for manipulation, thereby affecting the fairness of the game. CFTC Chairman Michael Selig stated that the regulator will "highly consider the opinions of the leagues" when evaluating such contracts, believing that sports leagues have a professional advantage in identifying potential manipulation risks. The NFL has not yet released the full text of the letter, and it remains unclear whether Kalshi and Polymarket will adjust their related products.

data.title: The U.S. Republican Party has introduced the "American Mining Act" to promote the expansion of mining and consolidate Bitcoin reserves.
data.description: U.S. Senators Cynthia Lummis and Bill Cassidy introduced the "Mined in America Act," aimed at promoting the development of the cryptocurrency mining industry and enshrining President Trump's executive order establishing a strategic Bitcoin reserve in law. The bill plans to strengthen the role of cryptocurrency mining in the U.S. economy while solidifying previous policy arrangements regarding the strategic Bitcoin reserve through legislation. This reserve primarily consists of Bitcoin seized by the U.S. Treasury Department through law enforcement. The bill also requires the U.S. Department of Commerce to establish a voluntary certification system to regulate mining pools and farms. Furthermore, certified mining farms must gradually reduce their reliance on mining equipment produced by companies associated with "foreign adversaries." The proponents stated that this bill is a key step in promoting the U.S. as a global digital asset center and aims to enhance the nation's strategic position in the cryptocurrency industry chain.

data.title: Cardano founder launches Midnight privacy blockchain, emphasizing ZooKeeper and compliance compatibility.
data.description: Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson announced the launch of Midnight, a privacy-oriented collaborative blockchain. The network has generated its genesis block, marking the project's official entry into operation. Developed by Input Output Global, Midnight is positioned as a "fourth-generation blockchain," aiming to promote the on-chaining of real-world assets through end-to-end privacy capabilities. The network employs zero-knowledge proof (ZK) technology to enable selective data disclosure, protecting user privacy while meeting compliance requirements. Architecturally, Midnight has an independent ledger, consensus mechanism, and smart contract environment, and connects to the Cardano ecosystem through interoperability design. Its hybrid ledger supports the simultaneous processing of public and private data in the same transaction, and provides the ability to conceal balances and counterparty information. Furthermore, Midnight introduces a programming language called Compact to lower the barrier for developers to build privacy applications and adopts a dual-token model to optimize network usage costs and incentive mechanisms.

data.title: An American man has been indicted in connection with the Uranium Finance hack, which involved $54 million and could face up to 30 years in prison.
data.description: U.S. prosecutors have formally charged Jonathan Spalletta, a Maryland man, with multiple attacks on the decentralized exchange Uranium Finance since 2021, involving approximately $54 million. According to the indictment released by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York, Spalletta faces charges of computer fraud and money laundering, carrying maximum sentences of 10 and 20 years in prison respectively, for a combined maximum sentence of 30 years. Prosecutors allege that Spalletta manipulated smart contract trading processes to create fictitious profits, illegally withdrawing more funds than he was entitled to, ultimately causing severe damage to and even the collapse of the exchange. Furthermore, the investigation indicates that he used some of the illicit proceeds to purchase collectibles, including a piece of Wright brothers airplane fabric that was taken to the moon by astronaut Neil Armstrong during the Apollo 11 mission. Prosecutors emphasized that crypto assets are also protected by law, and that "the notion that 'cryptocurrencies are just virtual assets' cannot be used as an excuse for theft."

data.title: Atlas Scout Program Engages Columbia Students with Insightful Discussions
data.description:
A recent event at Columbia University attracted a full house of students eager to engage with the Atlas Scout Program. YZi Labs posted on X that the session featured sharp questions from attendees, reflecting the program's aim to inspire future innovators. Ella Zhang addressed the audience, emphasizing the importance of dreaming and building for future civilizations, and expressed YZi Labs' desire to be a primary contact for aspiring leaders.

The event was co-hosted by Blockchain at CU and the Columbia Investment & Finance Society (CIFS). Cannae Huang, President of CIFS, provided an overview of the BNB ecosystem, while insights on stablecoins were shared by a lead from BNB Chain. The program's next stop is Harvard University.

data.title: University Students Encouraged to Apply for Opportunities
data.description: Students from prestigious universities such as Stanford, Harvard, MIT, Columbia University, NYU, CMU, and UC Berkeley are being encouraged to apply for new opportunities. YZi Labs posted on X, highlighting the initiative aimed at engaging students from these institutions. The call to action is directed at those studying at these universities or individuals who know someone attending these schools. This initiative seeks to leverage the talent and potential of students from top educational institutions, offering them a platform to explore new avenues for growth and development.

data.title: Microsoft Enhances Copilot Researcher with Critique and Council Features
data.description: Microsoft has introduced two new features, Critique and Council, to its Copilot Researcher tool. According to NS3.AI, the Critique workflow achieved a score of 57.4 points on the DRACO benchmark. Critique utilizes GPT for drafting and Claude for reviewing, while Council operates both models simultaneously and employs a judge model to evaluate the outputs.
